concepts.name: radial basis function
concepts.description: Basis function hₘ(x) = D(||x - μₘ||) depending only on distance from center μₘ.

concepts.name: wavelet basis
concepts.description: A basis of wavelet functions for representing signals, allowing multi-resolution analysis.

concepts.name: adaptive wavelet filtering
concepts.description: A wavelet smoothing method that adaptively selects coefficients based on data-driven criteria.

concepts.name: kernel smoother
concepts.description: A nonparametric regression method using kernel-weighted local averages.

concepts.name: penalization method
concepts.description: A formulation of SVMs as optimization with a penalty term, connecting SVMs to regularization approaches.

concepts.name: supervised learning
concepts.description:
The ﬁrst three examples described in Chapter 1 have several components
in common. For each there is a set of variables that might be denoted as
inputs, which are measured or preset. These have some inﬂuence on one or
more outputs. For each example the goal is to use the inputs to predict the
values of the outputs. This exercise is called supervised learning.
We have used the more modern language of machine learning. In the
statistical literature the inputs are often called the predictors, a term we
will use interchangeably with inputs, and more classically the independent
variables. In the pattern recognition literature the term features is preferred,
which we use as well. The outputs are called the responses, or classically
the dependent variables.

concepts.name: training data
concepts.description:
ith observation from the N-vector xj consisting of all the observations on
variable Xj. Since all vectors are assumed to be column vectors, the ith
row of X is xT
i , the vector transpose of xi.
For the moment we can loosely state the learning task as follows: given
the value of an input vector X, make a good prediction of the output Y,
denoted by ˆY (pronounced “y-hat”). If Y takes values in IR then so should
ˆY ; likewise for categorical outputs, ˆG should take values in the same set G
associated with G.
For a two-class G, one approach is to denote the binary coded target
as Y , and then treat it as a quantitative output. The predictions ˆY will
typically lie in [0, 1], and we can assign to ˆG the class label according to
whether ˆy > 0.5. This approach generalizes to K-level qualitative outputs
as well.
We need data to construct prediction rules, often a lot of it. We thus
suppose we have available a set of measurements (xi, yi) or (xi, gi), i =
1, . . . , N, known as the training data, with which to construct our prediction
rule.

concepts.name: bias in machine learning
concepts.description:
The term ˆβ0 is the intercept, also known as the bias in machine learning.
Often it is convenient to include the constant variable 1 in X, include ˆβ0 in
the vector of coeﬃcients ˆβ, and then write the linear model in vector form
as an inner product
ˆY = XT ˆβ,
(2.2)

concepts.name: regression function
concepts.description:
the conditional expectation, also known as the regression function. Thus
the best prediction of Y at any point X = x is the conditional mean, when
best is measured by average squared error.
The nearest-neighbor methods attempt to directly implement this recipe
using the training data. At each point x, we might ask for the average of all

concepts.name: Bayes rate
concepts.description:
This reasonable solution is known as the Bayes classiﬁer, and says that
we classify to the most probable class, using the conditional (discrete) dis-
tribution Pr(G|X). Figure 2.5 shows the Bayes-optimal decision boundary
for our simulation example. The error rate of the Bayes classiﬁer is called
the Bayes rate.

concepts.name: Bayes classifier
concepts.description:
This reasonable solution is known as the Bayes classiﬁer, and says that
we classify to the most probable class, using the conditional (discrete) dis-
tribution Pr(G|X). Figure 2.5 shows the Bayes-optimal decision boundary
for our simulation example. The error rate of the Bayes classiﬁer is called
the Bayes rate.

concepts.name: activation function
concepts.description:
where σ(x) = 1/(1 + e−x) is known as the activation function. Here, as
in the projection pursuit model, the directions αm and the bias terms bm
have to be determined, and their estimation is the meat of the computation.
Details are give in Chapter 11.
These adaptively chosen basis function methods are also known as dictio-
nary methods, where one has available a possibly inﬁnite set or dictionary
D of candidate basis functions from which to choose, and models are built
up by employing some kind of search mechanism.

concepts.name: loss function
concepts.description: Function L(Y, f(X)) that penalizes prediction errors, measuring cost of predicting f(X) when true value is Y.

concepts.name: squared error loss
concepts.description: Loss function L(Y, f(X)) = (Y - f(X))², the most common choice for quantitative responses.

concepts.name: absolute error loss
concepts.description: Loss function L(Y, f(X)) = |Y - f(X)|, less sensitive to outliers than squared error.

concepts.name: conditional expectation
concepts.description: E[Y|X=x], the expected value of Y given X=x, which minimizes expected squared error loss.

concepts.name: conditional median
concepts.description: The median of Y given X=x, which minimizes expected absolute error loss.

concepts.name: bayes decision boundary
concepts.description: The boundary between regions where the Bayes classifier assigns different classes.

concepts.name: least squares fitting
concepts.description: Method for fitting linear models by minimizing residual sum of squares.

concepts.name: residual sum of squares
concepts.description: RSS(β) = Σ(yi - xᵢᵀβ)², the criterion minimized in least squares fitting.

concepts.name: fitted values
concepts.description: Predictions ŷ = Xβ̂ from a fitted linear model on the training data.

concepts.name: k-nearest neighbor averaging
concepts.description: Method that predicts by averaging the response values of the k closest training points.

concepts.name: k-nearest neighbor classifier
concepts.description: Classification method assigning to the majority class among k nearest training neighbors.

concepts.name: nearest neighbor decision boundary
concepts.description: The piecewise linear or irregular boundary formed by nearest neighbor classification.

concepts.name: curse of dimensionality
concepts.description: Phenomenon where nearest neighbors become far away in high dimensions, requiring exponentially more data for local methods.

concepts.name: sampling density
concepts.description: Density of observations per unit volume in input space, which decreases exponentially with dimension.

concepts.name: empty space phenomenon
concepts.description: In high dimensions, most of the volume of a hypercube is near the boundaries, leaving the interior nearly empty.

concepts.name: structured regression model
concepts.description: Regression model imposing constraints on the functional form to reduce complexity and avoid overfitting.

concepts.name: linear model assumption
concepts.description: Assumption that regression function is linear in inputs: f(X) = Xᵀβ.

concepts.name: additive model
concepts.description: Model where f(X) = Σfⱼ(Xⱼ), a sum of univariate functions of each predictor.

concepts.name: linear combination model
concepts.description: Model where f(X) = g(αᵀX), applying a function to a linear combination of inputs.

concepts.name: interaction term
concepts.description: Product or other combination of predictors, allowing departure from pure additivity.

concepts.name: penalty functional
concepts.description: Functional J(f) measuring function complexity, large for rapidly varying functions.

concepts.name: smoothing parameter
concepts.description: Parameter λ controlling tradeoff between fit to data and roughness penalty.

concepts.name: kernel width
concepts.description: Parameter controlling the size of the neighborhood in kernel methods, analogous to k in k-NN.

concepts.name: nadaraya-watson estimator
concepts.description: Kernel-weighted average for regression: f̂(x) = Σ Kλ(x, xᵢ)yᵢ / Σ Kλ(x, xᵢ).

concepts.name: locally weighted regression
concepts.description: Regression fit by weighted least squares with kernel weights based on distance from target point.

concepts.name: basis function expansion
concepts.description: Representing f(X) = Σ hₘ(X)βₘ as linear combination of fixed basis functions hₘ.

concepts.name: sigmoid basis function
concepts.description: Basis function hₘ(x) = σ(αₘᵀx + bₘ) using sigmoid activation, as in neural networks.

concepts.name: dictionary
concepts.description: Collection D of candidate basis functions from which to build a model.

concepts.name: model complexity
concepts.description: Measure of flexibility of a model class, affecting bias-variance tradeoff.

concepts.name: degrees of freedom
concepts.description: Effective number of parameters in a model, measuring model complexity.

concepts.name: irreducible error
concepts.description: Error due to intrinsic variability in Y that cannot be reduced by any prediction method.

concepts.name: optimism of training error
concepts.description: Tendency of training error to underestimate true test error, increasing with model complexity.

concepts.name: out-of-sample error
concepts.description: Error measured on data not used for training.

concepts.name: expected test error
concepts.description: The expected value of test error over all possible training sets of fixed size.

concepts.name: pointwise prediction error
concepts.description: Prediction error at a specific point x₀ in the input space.

concepts.name: misclassification error
concepts.description: Probability of incorrect classification, equivalent to expected 0-1 loss.

concepts.name: posterior probability
concepts.description: Pr(G=k|X=x), probability of class k given input x.

concepts.name: nearest neighbor region
concepts.description: Set of points closer to a given training point than to any other training point, forming Voronoi tessellation.

concepts.name: k-nearest neighbor boundary
concepts.description: Decision boundary formed by k-NN classifier, which becomes smoother as k increases.

concepts.name: dimension reduction
concepts.description: Techniques to reduce the dimensionality of the input space before applying prediction methods.

concepts.name: projection method
concepts.description: Method that projects high-dimensional data onto lower-dimensional subspace.

concepts.name: least squares projection
concepts.description: Fitting by least squares viewed as projection onto a linear subspace.

concepts.name: model selection criterion
concepts.description: Criterion for choosing among models, balancing fit and complexity.

concepts.name: training set validation
concepts.description: Using held-out data to estimate test error and select models.

concepts.name: cross-validation error
concepts.description: Estimate of test error obtained by cross-validation.

concepts.name: prior distribution
concepts.description: Distribution over function parameters representing beliefs before seeing data.

concepts.name: bayesian framework
concepts.description: Approach treating parameters as random and computing posterior distributions.

concepts.name: squared bias
concepts.description: Component of prediction error equal to squared difference between expected prediction and true function, decreases with model complexity.

concepts.name: variance of prediction
concepts.description: Component of prediction error measuring variability of predictions across training sets, increases with model complexity.

concepts.name: Lasso
concepts.description:
FIGURE 3.15. Left panel shows the LAR coeﬃcient proﬁles on the simulated
data, as a function of the L1 arc length. The right panel shows the Lasso proﬁle.
They are identical until the dark-blue coeﬃcient crosses zero at an arc length of
about 18.

concepts.name: gauss-markov theorem
concepts.description: States that the least squares estimates of parameters have the smallest variance among all linear unbiased estimates.

concepts.name: orthogonal projection
concepts.description: The projection of y onto the column space of X, minimizing the residual sum of squares.

concepts.name: qr decomposition
concepts.description: A matrix factorization that decomposes a matrix into an orthogonal matrix Q and an upper triangular matrix R, used as a numerically stable method for least squares.

concepts.name: gram-schmidt orthogonalization
concepts.description: A procedure for constructing an orthogonal basis from a set of vectors by sequential orthogonalization.

concepts.name: best subset selection
concepts.description: For each k, finds the subset of size k that gives the smallest residual sum of squares.

concepts.name: leaps and bounds procedure
concepts.description: An efficient algorithm for best subset selection that makes it feasible for p as large as 30 or 40.

concepts.name: forward stepwise selection
concepts.description: Starts with the intercept and sequentially adds the predictor that most improves the fit, producing a greedy nested sequence of models.

concepts.name: backward stepwise selection
concepts.description: Starts with the full model and sequentially deletes the predictor with the smallest Z-score (least impact on fit).

concepts.name: forward stagewise regression
concepts.description: Starts with all coefficients zero, identifies the variable most correlated with residual, computes simple linear regression coefficient of residual on that variable, and adds it to current coefficient without adjusting other variables.

concepts.name: ridge regression
concepts.description: Shrinks regression coefficients by imposing an L2 penalty on their size, controlled by parameter lambda.

concepts.name: basis pursuit
concepts.description: The signal processing name for the lasso method.

concepts.name: soft thresholding
concepts.description: Lasso's coefficient shrinkage rule that translates each coefficient by a constant, truncating at zero.

concepts.name: least angle regression
concepts.description: A democratic version of forward stepwise that enters 'as much' of a predictor as it deserves, moving coefficients toward least squares continuously while keeping correlations tied.

concepts.name: lar-lasso connection
concepts.description: LAR with a simple modification produces the entire lasso path: drop variables when their coefficients hit zero.

concepts.name: homotopy algorithm
concepts.description: An algorithm for computing piecewise-linear lasso path discovered by Osborne et al.

concepts.name: degrees of freedom for adaptive fit
concepts.description: df(y-hat) = sum of Cov(y-hat_i, y_i) / sigma^2, measuring how hard a model fits the data.

concepts.name: principal components regression
concepts.description: Forms derived inputs as principal components of X, then regresses y on the first M components.

concepts.name: partial least squares
concepts.description: Constructs linear combinations of inputs for regression using both X and y, weighting inputs by their univariate effect on y.

concepts.name: canonical correlation analysis
concepts.description: Finds uncorrelated linear combinations of inputs and outputs with successively maximized correlations.

concepts.name: reduced-rank regression
concepts.description: Regression method that constrains coefficient matrix to have low rank, borrowing strength among multiple responses via CCA truncation.

concepts.name: curds and whey
concepts.description: A shrinkage method for multiple outputs that shrinks canonical variates between X and Y, a smooth version of reduced rank regression.

concepts.name: incremental forward stagewise regression
concepts.description: Forward stagewise with small step size epsilon, updating coefficient of most correlated variable by epsilon*sign(correlation) repeatedly.

concepts.name: infinitesimal forward stagewise regression
concepts.description: The limiting procedure as step size epsilon approaches zero in incremental forward stagewise, also called FS0 or L2boost.

concepts.name: l2boost
concepts.description: Another name for infinitesimal forward stagewise regression due to its connections to boosting.

concepts.name: dantzig selector
concepts.description: Minimizes L1 norm of coefficients subject to maximum absolute correlation of residuals with predictors being bounded.

concepts.name: grouped lasso
concepts.description: Lasso variant for pre-defined groups of predictors that encourages sparsity at both group and individual levels using group-wise L2 norms.

concepts.name: relaxed lasso
concepts.description: Two-stage lasso: first run lasso to select predictors, then apply lasso again only to selected predictors with separate penalty parameter to reduce bias.

concepts.name: scad penalty
concepts.description: Smoothly clipped absolute deviation penalty that shrinks large coefficients less severely than lasso to reduce bias.

concepts.name: adaptive lasso
concepts.description: Weighted lasso penalty using weights w_j = 1/|beta-hat_j|^nu from OLS, yielding consistent parameter estimates while maintaining convexity.

concepts.name: pathwise coordinate descent
concepts.description: Algorithm for computing lasso solution by fixing penalty parameter and optimizing successively over each parameter while holding others fixed.

concepts.name: z-score for regression coefficients
concepts.description: The standardized coefficient z_j = beta-hat_j / se(beta-hat_j) used for hypothesis testing, distributed as t_{N-p-1} under null hypothesis.

concepts.name: f-statistic for nested models
concepts.description: Test statistic for comparing nested linear models, measuring change in RSS per additional parameter normalized by error variance estimate.

concepts.name: variance-covariance matrix of least squares estimates
concepts.description: Var(beta-hat) = (X^T X)^{-1} sigma^2, giving the sampling variability of parameter estimates.

concepts.name: multiple regression coefficient interpretation
concepts.description: The jth coefficient represents the additional contribution of x_j on y after x_j has been adjusted for all other predictors.

concepts.name: multicollinearity
concepts.description: When predictors are highly correlated, coefficients become unstable with high variance, as small residual vectors lead to imprecise estimates.

concepts.name: bias-variance tradeoff in model selection
concepts.description: Shrinkage and selection methods sacrifice some bias to reduce variance, potentially improving overall prediction accuracy.

concepts.name: mean squared error decomposition
concepts.description: MSE = variance + (bias)^2, showing tradeoff between variance and bias in estimation.

concepts.name: aic criterion
concepts.description: A criterion for model selection that accounts for the number of parameters fit, used to choose tuning parameters.

concepts.name: l1 arc length
concepts.description: The total L1 distance traveled along a coefficient path, summing L1 norms of coefficient changes at each step.

concepts.name: piecewise-linear solution path
concepts.description: Property of LAR and lasso where coefficient profiles are piecewise linear as tuning parameter varies, enabling efficient computation.

concepts.name: soft-thresholding operator
concepts.description: An operator S(t, λ) = sign(t)(|t|−λ)+ used in coordinate descent for lasso, which shrinks coefficients toward zero by the amount λ and sets them to zero if below the threshold.
